report_name: Microsoft Attributes Mastra NPM Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Actor Sapphire Sleet

threat_actors.name: Sapphire Sleet
threat_actors.description: A North Korean state-sponsored threat actor that executed a supply chain attack against the Mastra open-source AI framework by compromising an NPM maintainer's account to publish malicious packages.
threat_actors.goals: To compromise developer systems for stealing sensitive credentials (API keys, SSH keys) and intellectual property, with an ultimate goal of financial gain.

attack_patterns.name: Valid Accounts
attack_patterns.description: The threat actor compromised the 'ehindero' NPM maintainer account, likely through phishing or credential stuffing, to gain the necessary permissions to publish malicious package updates.

attack_patterns.name: Compromise Software Supply Chain
attack_patterns.description: The attackers used a compromised NPM maintainer account to publish 141 malicious versions of official Mastra framework packages, which included a malicious dependency.

attack_patterns.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location
attack_patterns.description: The malicious dependency was named 'easy-day-js', a deliberate typosquat of the legitimate and widely-used 'dayjs' library, to deceive developers and automated build systems.

malware.name: easy-day-js
malware.description: A malicious typosquatted NPM package, impersonating the legitimate 'dayjs' library. It was added as a dependency to compromised Mastra packages to infect developer systems and facilitate credential theft.

identities.name: Mastra
identities.description: A popular open-source TypeScript framework for building AI agents that was targeted in a software supply chain attack.

identities.name: ehindero
identities.description: The NPM maintainer account that was compromised by Sapphire Sleet to publish malicious packages.

identities.name: sergey2016
identities.description: An NPM account, likely controlled by the threat actor, used to stage a 'clean' version of the malicious 'easy-day-js' package before the main attack.

infrastructure.name: Compromised NPM Maintainer Account 'ehindero'
infrastructure.description: The compromised NPM account 'ehindero' was used by the attackers to publish malicious updates to 141 Mastra packages.

infrastructure.name: NPM Staging Account 'sergey2016'
infrastructure.description: The NPM account 'sergey2016' was used to publish a 'clean' version of the malicious 'easy-day-js' package a day before the main attack, likely to pre-populate registries.

indicators.name: Malicious NPM Package easy-day-js
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Malicious NPM Package easy-day-js Directory
id: 7c5b9f8a-1b2c-4d5e-8f6a-7b8c9d0e1f2a
status: experimental
description: Detects the presence of the malicious 'easy-day-js' NPM package directory within node_modules.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/north-korean-hackers-blamed-for-mastra-npm-supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\node_modules\easy-day-js\'
      - '/node_modules/easy-day-js/'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the presence of the malicious 'easy-day-js' NPM package, which indicates a software supply chain compromise.

indicators.name: NPM Dependency Check for easy-day-js
indicators.pattern:
title: NPM Dependency Check for easy-day-js
id: 3e4f5a6b-7c8d-4e9f-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects command-line execution of 'npm ls' to check for the malicious 'easy-day-js' package, as suggested for hunting.
references:
  - https://www.securityweek.com/north-korean-hackers-blamed-for-mastra-npm-supply-chain-attack/
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-06-22
tags:
  - attack.t1195.002
level: medium
logsource:
  category: process_creation
detection:
  selection:
    CommandLine|contains|all:
      - 'npm'
      - ' ls '
      - 'easy-day-js'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects a command-line pattern used to hunt for the presence of the malicious 'easy-day-js' package in a project's dependency tree.

relationships.target_name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location
